title: Singular Points on Positroid Varieties and Physics Applications
speaker: Joseph Fluegemann
abstract: We heard last week in Daoji's talk about positroid varieties, which are subvarieties in the Grassmannian defined by cyclic rank conditions, and which are related to Schubert varieties. In this talk, we will provide a criterion for whether positroid varieties are singular at certain distinguished points, and we will show that this information is sufficient to determine whether an entire positroid variety is smooth or singular. This will involve looking at combinatorial diagrams called "affine pipe dreams." We can also form a partial order on positroid varieties given by deletion and contraction, such that there is closure for smooth positroid varieties, and we will characterize the minimal singular elements in this order. Finally, we will discuss a couple applications to physics which involve the plabic diagrams introduced by Postnikov: the BCFW bridge decomposition and inverse soft factors.

proof_stages.label: Bounded-affine-permutation model of positroid varieties
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up Gr(k,n), its affine charts and torus fixed points indexed by lambda, and then defines positroid varieties through bounded affine permutations, cyclic rank conditions, and the matrix-to-permutation map.

proof_stages.label: Affine pipe dreams on a lambda-chart
proof_stages.summary: Classical pipe dreams and cross-elbow moves are reviewed, then replaced by affine pipe dreams whose outer shape is determined by the chosen chart lambda and whose crosses encode the bounded affine permutation of the positroid variety locally on U_lambda.

proof_stages.label: Smoothness from pipe-dream uniqueness
proof_stages.summary: Smoothness or singularity may be tested at torus fixed points, and the key criterion is proved by identifying the local multiplicity with a coefficient in equivariant cohomology, which the affine AJS/Billey formula evaluates as the number of affine pipe-dream representatives.

proof_stages.label: Maximal-rectangle rigidity criterion
proof_stages.summary: The uniqueness criterion is made checkable by showing that every cross/near-miss move lies in a maximal rectangle and that a rectangular pipe dream is rigid exactly when it reduces to northwest and southeast partitions.

proof_stages.label: Deletion-contraction order and singularity monotonicity
proof_stages.summary: Deletion and contraction are defined as fixed-point intersections for one-coordinate torus actions and translated into pipe-dream operations, yielding a partial order in which smoothness is preserved downward and singularity upward.

proof_stages.label: Atomic minimal singular pairs
proof_stages.summary: The minimal singular elements in the deletion-contraction order are identified as atomic positroid pairs: after any deletion or contraction they become smooth, so their only nonrigidity is one cross moving freely along a diagonal.

proof_stages.label: Amplitude-diagram applications
proof_stages.summary: The speaker relates positroid varieties and affine pipe dreams to N=4 supersymmetric Yang-Mills amplitudes: plabic diagrams give bounded affine permutations, top affine pipe dreams encode BCFW bridge decompositions, and inverse soft factors behave like inverse deletion/contraction operations relevant to factorization.
